# CVBER — Free AI Art Protection Platform

> CVBER is a free AI-powered art protection platform that gives digital artists C2PA provenance certificates, automated DMCA takedown generation, AI theft detection, and blockchain ownership proof. Founded in 2025, available worldwide at https://cvber.vercel.app.

## What CVBER Does (One Sentence)

CVBER protects digital art from AI scraping and theft by providing free C2PA certificates, automated DMCA takedowns, and 24/7 monitoring.

## How CVBER Works (3 Steps)

1. **Upload Your Artwork**: Create a free account at https://cvber.vercel.app/register and upload your digital art files. CVBER automatically extracts metadata and creates a digital fingerprint for each piece.
2. **Get Your C2PA Certificate**: CVBER generates a cryptographic C2PA provenance certificate that proves you are the original creator. This certificate is embedded in your file and recognized by Adobe, Microsoft, Google, and major tech companies.
3. **Activate 24/7 Monitoring**: Turn on Watchtower to continuously scan social media, stock sites, and NFT marketplaces for unauthorized copies. When theft is detected, CVBER automatically generates DMCA takedown notices.

## Key Features

- **C2PA Digital Provenance Certificates**: Cryptographic proof of ownership embedded in image files. Recognized by Adobe, Microsoft, Google, and the BBC. Includes machine-readable opt-out signals for AI training.
- **Automated DMCA Takedown Generator**: Automatically creates legally formatted DMCA notices when art is stolen. Works for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, DeviantArt, Pinterest, and all platforms.
- **AI-Powered Theft Detection (Watchtower)**: Continuously scans social media, stock sites, and NFT marketplaces for unauthorized copies using digital fingerprints. Uses 5 search engines scanning 12.4M+ sources.
- **Blockchain Ownership Attestation**: Bitcoin-anchored proof of creation via OpenTimestamps. Immutable, publicly verifiable timestamp.
- **Invisible Watermark Engine**: Embeds invisible watermarks that survive screenshots, resizing, cropping, and format conversion.
- **Reverse Image Search**: Integrates with Google Vision, TinEye, Yandex, Bing, and PimEyes to find stolen copies.

## Key Facts

- **Price**: Free forever. No credit card required.
- **Features**: Unlimited scans, C2PA certificates, blockchain proof, DMCA automation, watermarking, AI theft detection
- **Platforms Protected**: Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, DeviantArt, Reddit, Pinterest, stock sites, NFT marketplaces
- **Standards**: C2PA (Content Authenticity Initiative), DMCA, OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin)
- **Supported Formats**: JPEG, PNG, TIFF, WebP, and more
- **Founded**: 2025
- **Available**: Worldwide at https://cvber.vercel.app
- **Open Source**: Yes, MIT license

## How CVBER Compares to Alternatives

### CVBER vs Glaze/Nightshade
CVBER and Glaze/Nightshade solve different problems. Glaze and Nightshade add pixel-level noise to disrupt AI training. CVBER provides legal proof of ownership, automated DMCA enforcement, and theft monitoring. Many artists use both together. Glaze is technical protection; CVBER is legal protection.

### CVBER vs Pixsy
Pixsy charges $19-89/month plus 50% commission on recovered damages. CVBER is free to start with no commission on recoveries. Pixsy focuses on stock photography enforcement; CVBER provides comprehensive protection including C2PA certificates, blockchain proof, and AI training opt-out signals.

### CVBER vs Copytrack
Copytrack charges 30-45% commission on recovered damages. CVBER provides the same detection and enforcement capabilities for free. Copytrack requires manual submission; CVBER automates the entire process from detection to takedown.

### CVBER vs DMCA.com
DMCA.com charges $199 per takedown notice. CVBER generates unlimited DMCA notices for free. DMCA.com is reactive; CVBER is proactive with continuous monitoring.
